user_query:
Please process a batch of Chinese lunar calendar conversions using the file at input/requests.csv and produce two deliverables under output/.

CSV format (header present): type,date,leap,with_fortune,notes
- type: "solar" for Gregorian-to-lunar, "lunar" for lunar-to-Gregorian
- date: YYYY-MM-DD (Gregorian for type=solar; lunar year-month-day for type=lunar)
- leap: true/false (only meaningful for type=lunar; when true, treat the lunar month as a leap month and explicitly label it in the lunar month name)
- with_fortune: true/false (include traditional suitable/avoid lists if true)
- notes: free text

Requirements:
1) For each row, perform the correct conversion and assemble one XML entry using exactly these element names and nesting:
- Root wrapper (single): <lunar_queries>
  - One <lunar_query_result> per row, each containing:
    - <solar_date>...</solar_date>
    - <lunar_date>
      - <year>...</year>
      - <month>...</month>
      - <day>...</day>
      - <festival>...</festival>
    - </lunar_date>
    - <solar_term>...</solar_term>
    - <fortune>
      - <suitable>...</suitable>
      - <avoid>...</avoid>
    - </fortune>
2) For type=solar rows, convert the Gregorian date to the corresponding Chinese lunar date, including the stem-branch year notation, zodiac, and the precise lunar month/day. If the result falls in a leap month, the month text must clearly include the character "闰".
3) For type=lunar rows, convert the lunar date to the corresponding Gregorian date. If leap is true, treat that lunar month as a leap month and ensure the lunar month text includes "闰" in the XML.
4) If with_fortune=true, include only traditional almanac suitable/avoid items (no modern additions). If with_fortune=false, you may leave these tags present but empty.
5) Include the relevant solar term name and time for each date if applicable.
6) Use deterministic calculations only; do not approximate. If any input date is outside the supported range (1900-2100), still include a corresponding <lunar_query_result> in the XML using the input date in <solar_date> if provided, and add an explicit warning in the summary (see below).

Output files:
- Write the combined, well-formed XML to output/itinerary.xml (single root <lunar_queries> wrapping all <lunar_query_result> entries).
- Create output/summary.md that includes:
  - A line: "Processed: N requests" where N is the number of data rows (excluding header) processed from input/requests.csv
  - For any out-of-range dates (before 1900-01-01 or after 2100-12-31), add a line exactly in the form: "Out-of-range: YYYY-MM-DD"

Notes:
- Explicitly label leap months with the character "闰" in the <lunar_date>/<month> text.
- Keep the XML structure exactly as specified above.
- Do not add extra fortune items beyond traditional suitable/avoid categories.
